jokes.text: Jason, have you ever put out a cigar on Gilbert Gottfried's neck? Because I have, and his screams were the worst thing I'd ever heard. Until tonight.
jokes.setup: Jenna seemingly giving encouragement to Jason on a talent show
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's actually insulting his performance by comparing it to Gilbert Gottfried's screams of pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - starts with fake encouragement, escalates to absurd celebrity violence anecdote, then delivers devastating insult. Character-specific dark humor with excellent misdirection.

jokes.text: Also, Jason, if you think you're passing for straight, you're embarrassing yourself.
jokes.setup: Jenna rolling her ankle and being in pain
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to casually deliver another harsh critique of Jason
jokes.explanation: Shows Jenna's relentless cruelty - even physical pain doesn't stop her from insulting contestants. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Did you know that both her mothers are serial killers?
jokes.setup: Explaining why audience loves Shayla's personal story
jokes.punchline: The absurdly dark 'personal story' of having two serial killer mothers
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist escalation. Takes reality TV sob story trope to ridiculous extreme with perfect economy.

jokes.text: That's America.
jokes.setup: Learning about the serial killer mothers
jokes.punchline: Liz's weary, resigned commentary on American entertainment values
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon deadpan - captures her cynical worldview in two words. Maximum economy, character-specific delivery.

jokes.text: A little less than my kidnapped wife, but I did get a nice Christmas card from Avery and Kim Jong-Un.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if he missed her over the break
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of wife's kidnapping and Christmas card from North Korean dictator
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack line - treats international kidnapping casually while finding absurd silver lining. Perfect character voice with dark comedy escalation.

jokes.text: She's like a little human tumbler of scotch.
jokes.setup: Talking about quality time with his daughter Liddy
jokes.punchline: Compares his baby daughter to his favorite alcoholic beverage
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Jack - shows affection for daughter through alcohol metaphor. Perfectly character-specific and highly quotable. Sweet yet twisted.

jokes.text: If you want, I can recommend a good gynecologist. You know, 'cause I really like my guy. He's sort of a doogie howser type. But younger.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Jack is going soft
jokes.punchline: Escalates from suggesting gynecologist to impossibly young doctor
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdism - the 'younger than Doogie Howser' escalation is perfectly illogical in his voice.

jokes.text: You took the train to your parents' house. On Christmas Eve, you forgot that eggnog has alcohol in it, and got into a shoving match with your aunt about who puts the star on top of the tree.
jokes.setup: Jack claiming to know exactly how Liz's holidays went
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and pathetic holiday scenario that's clearly accurate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack/Liz dynamic - he knows her pathetic life patterns precisely. The specificity makes it funnier and more believable.

jokes.text: It was my year! What, lupus lets you just cut the line?
jokes.setup: Defending her fight with aunt about Christmas tree star
jokes.punchline: Reveals the aunt has lupus and Liz's petty competitiveness about tree traditions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Liz moment - shows her petty competitiveness even toward sick relatives. Dark but character-perfect, great escalation.

jokes.text: Wrong, Jack. 'Cause they weren't sweaters. They were Dickies!
jokes.setup: Jack betting she's wearing unwanted Christmas sweaters out of guilt
jokes.punchline: Technically wins the bet on a ridiculous technicality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - she technically wins Jack's bet but on the lamest possible technicality. Very Liz.

jokes.text: The world is ending tomorrow! And you're happy about that? Oh, of course. I get to go to heaven and receive my reward! 72 virgin margaritas, hold the salt.
jokes.setup: Kenneth believing the world is ending and being happy about it
jokes.punchline: Mishears '72 virgins' as '72 virgin margaritas'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence - mishears Islamic paradise reference as cocktail order. Character-specific and sweet.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry I won't be seeing you in heaven, Mr. Spurlock. But on the bright side, black hell does have a jukebox.
jokes.setup: Kenneth apologizing for Tracy going to hell
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions segregated afterlife with amenities
jokes.explanation: Dark Kenneth humor - his innocent racism paired with trying to find silver lining in eternal damnation. Uncomfortable but character-consistent.

jokes.text: I came across the following quote on the side of a tampon box this Christmas. 'Work like you don't need the money. Love like you've never been hurt. Dance like nobody's watching. Enjoy these satchel Paige brand tampons.'
jokes.setup: Liz quoting inspirational saying
jokes.punchline: Reveals it came from tampon box with absurd endorsement
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor about corporate inspiration. Sets up episode title reference. Moderately funny but not exceptional.

jokes.text: When you Google 'Jenna Maroney' now, I come up first. Not the Jenna Maroney who electrocuted all those horses.
jokes.setup: Jenna celebrating her Google search prominence
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to horse electrocution, then reveals it was also her
jokes.explanation: Great Jenna moment - proudly distinguishing herself from terrible act she also committed. Perfect character logic and dark humor.

jokes.text: This has nothing to do with Jenna's success that I'm jealous of, but if that yellow-haired bag of teeth keeps me waiting for rehearsal, I will set my dressing room on fire.
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming he's not jealous
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves jealousy with elaborate insult and arson threat
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy jealousy - denies it while immediately proving it. 'Yellow-haired bag of teeth' is creative insult.

jokes.text: I'm not doing this. But I'm acting out. Right. And I know it'll all blow over eventually, and so I'm just going to skip the exhausting middle part.
jokes.setup: Tracy starting to act out for attention
jokes.punchline: Meta-awareness of sitcom patterns and decision to skip his own subplot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - Tracy recognizes his own sitcom behavior patterns and opts out. Very 30 Rock self-awareness.

jokes.text: I took a real age test. It said I'm dead.
jokes.setup: Liz calling him a 42-year-old man
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery of impossible test result
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - takes fake medical test seriously and reports impossible result matter-of-factly. Great economy.

jokes.text: Unlike cash cow, the failed NBC spin-off of cash cab. You try riding a cow through midtown Manhattan, Lemon. The animal will panic.
jokes.setup: Talking about money-making show being a 'cash cow'
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation leads to absurd failed show concept
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay escalation - takes metaphor literally to create absurd TV concept. Jack's serious delivery sells it.

jokes.text: What if I told you your first match burned his groin off in an accident at his cake shop.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to interest Liz in dating site
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and unsexy dating prospect
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail - the specificity of 'groin' and 'cake shop' makes it funnier. Fits Jack's poor understanding of what women want.

jokes.text: Even if you could sing, with that face it would be like eating a steak that just came out of a dumpster. I've done that.
jokes.setup: Jenna insulting child contestant Liddy
jokes.punchline: Disgusting metaphor followed by casual admission of dumpster dining
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna cruelty with perfect character detail. The dumpster steak admission reveals her desperation while maintaining cruelty.

jokes.text: Go jump back up your mother.
jokes.setup: Jenna trying out catchphrases
jokes.punchline: Anatomically impossible insult delivered to child
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist insult that's memorable and inappropriate. Very quotable but structurally simple.

jokes.text: Even if you could sing, with that face it would be like eating a steak that just came out of a dumpster. I've done that.
jokes.setup: Jenna insulting child's appearance
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of eating dumpster steak
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that reveals Jenna's desperation while delivering a brutal insult to a child

jokes.text: Sir, this chiquita banana sticker had been stuck to that ceiling for years. So I finally scraped it off.
jokes.setup: Kenneth doing something with a ladder
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been obsessed with removing banana sticker for years
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth obsessive-compulsive behavior. The specificity of 'Chiquita banana sticker' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: organize snack table by food jewishness
jokes.setup: Reading Kenneth's dream chores list
jokes.punchline: Absurd organizational system based on religious food laws
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth logic - treats kosher laws as organizational tool. Maximum economy and perfectly character-specific.

jokes.text: From now on everyone has to address me as 'the gentleman formerly known as rectum.'
jokes.setup: Tracy threatening to change his name for attention
jokes.punchline: Parodies Prince's name change with crude body part
jokes.explanation: Good Prince parody with Tracy's crude sensibility. Somewhat cheap but fits character's attention-seeking behavior.

jokes.text: I just realized, I haven't paid taxes in 30 years.
jokes.setup: Tracy listing self-destructive things he'll do
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of massive tax evasion
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy wealthy celebrity cluelessness. Matter-of-fact delivery of serious crime makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I started a camp for underprivileged kids last summer. We have to drive upstate to see if any of them are still alive.
jokes.setup: Tracy remembering he started a camp
jokes.punchline: Casual concern about children's survival
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy irresponsibility - starting charity then forgetting about it completely.

jokes.text: I haven't seen such a unanimously negative response since the Frasier spin-off, hey, Roz.
jokes.setup: Jack describing negative focus group results
jokes.punchline: References terrible NBC spin-off with awful title
jokes.explanation: Good NBC self-roasting. The casual delivery and awful title make it funnier.

jokes.text: So whoever you are, show me Jack's penis.
jokes.setup: Jenna thinking people are switching minds
jokes.punchline: Bizarre test to prove identity
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist Jenna logic. Direct and character-appropriate but somewhat crude.

jokes.text: Uh, also, FYI, women's hell is the same as aroused dog heaven.
jokes.setup: Kenneth warning Liz about women's hell
jokes.punchline: Disturbing theological detail about afterlife geography
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth darkness - delivers disturbing theological concept as helpful FYI. Perfect character voice with shocking content.

jokes.text: Only while playing tennis. I'm kidding. I've never played tennis.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's ever been in love
jokes.punchline: Misdirection about tennis love, then reveals he's never played
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with perfect Kenneth innocence. The tennis love pun setup works well.

jokes.text: In the words of my father, 'you deserve to be disappointed. Merry Christmas.'
jokes.setup: Liz being unusually mean to Kenneth about his beliefs
jokes.punchline: Quotes father's harsh Christmas wisdom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark family wisdom. Perfectly explains Liz's cynicism while being both cruel and funny. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: That's what Paul and I do to maintain intimacy when he's having his period.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Tracy spy on Liz
jokes.punchline: Reveals she treats her boyfriend like a woman during emotional cycles
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna relationship dysfunction. Shows her treating boyfriend according to her own dramatic logic.

jokes.text: Because I will not go back to putting hair extensions on dogs.
jokes.setup: Jenna worried about show being canceled
jokes.punchline: Reveals previous degrading work experience
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna career desperation backstory. Absurd enough to be funny, specific enough to be memorable.

jokes.text: You were trying to get me to commit suicide, right?
jokes.setup: Child singing to Jack
jokes.punchline: Jack interprets child's performance as suicide encouragement
jokes.explanation: Dark Jack humor - interprets innocent child performance as malicious. Good economy and character-specific cynicism.

jokes.text: Just because those kids had the same scared look on their faces that you had when that dog got too close to your stroller in the park.
jokes.setup: Jack talking to baby Liddy about business ethics
jokes.punchline: Connects business cruelty to specific parenting memory
jokes.explanation: Touching moment that's also funny - Jack's parental awakening through specific memory. Good character development.

jokes.text: Did you say 'money'? Is that your first word... money? Yes, money. Are you telling me that money's more important than doing what's right? I want money too!
jokes.setup: Baby Liddy trying to say 'mommy'
jokes.punchline: Jack consistently mishears it as business advice about money
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack moment - projects his business obsession onto baby's first word. Perfect character integration and escalation.

jokes.text: I mean, I'm the... I'm the one who had to nude-baptize all those teens.
jokes.setup: Kenneth expressing disappointment about wrong predictions
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of inappropriate religious ritual
jokes.explanation: Dark Kenneth humor - treats disturbing religious practice as normal disappointment. Perfect character obliviousness to inappropriateness.

jokes.text: Liz Lemon is a crack whore. Probably not, but continue.
jokes.setup: Tracy making dramatic accusation
jokes.punchline: Jenna's casual qualification while staying interested
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with Jenna's practical response to Tracy's conspiracy. Good timing and character voices.

jokes.text: And not the fun stationery store up on the Upper East Side. The skeezy one with trains.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing Penn Station
jokes.punchline: Confuses Penn Station with upscale stationery store
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy wealthy cluelessness. The specificity of Upper East Side stationery store makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Are you sure it was Liz Lemon and not present day Sally Field?
jokes.setup: Jenna questioning Tracy's identification
jokes.punchline: Comparing Liz to aged actress
jokes.explanation: Standard aging actress joke but fits Jenna's Hollywood perspective.

jokes.text: Her wrist was starting to bother her from slapping busboys.
jokes.setup: Explaining mother's joint pain medication
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of abusing service workers
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna family dysfunction - treats abuse as normal medical explanation. Character-specific casual cruelty.

jokes.text: The Manhattan center for penis enlargement? I know because my friend goes there. His name is Tracy.
jokes.setup: Guessing what building is near Penn Station
jokes.punchline: Reveals personal knowledge through transparent 'friend' lie
jokes.explanation: Decent Tracy oversharing with thin disguise. The 'friend named Tracy' is classic transparent lying.

jokes.text: Her wrist was starting to bother her from slapping busboys.
jokes.setup: Explanation for joint pain medication
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of abusive behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark character humor that reveals casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Oh, God! So much grapevining!
jokes.setup: Watching dance troupe performance
jokes.punchline: Jack's horrified reaction to basic dance move
jokes.explanation: Good Jack cultural disconnect - treats elementary dance move as horrifying spectacle. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: It's so beautiful. A mermaid! Dude, those are diapers.
jokes.setup: Kenneth seeing ocean for first time
jokes.punchline: Confuses floating garbage for mythical sea creature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence meets reality. His magical interpretation of pollution is sweet and funny.

jokes.text: You'll say you're seeing it ironically, and yet you'll tear up when Ashton Kutcher kisses Lea Michele. You know me. I love it when the swarthy girl gets the guy.
jokes.setup: Jack predicting Liz's movie experience
jokes.punchline: Liz admits Jack's right about her romantic movie weakness
jokes.explanation: Great Jack/Liz dynamic - he knows her guilty pleasures precisely. 'Swarthy girl' is perfect Liz self-description.

jokes.text: You've waited five weeks to see it, to make sure that you wouldn't contribute to it being the number one movie in America.
jokes.setup: Jack predicting Liz's movie plans
jokes.punchline: Accurate prediction of her contrarian timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight showing Jack's deep knowledge of Liz's contradictory nature

jokes.text: I love it when the swarthy girl gets the guy.
jokes.setup: Liz's response to Jack's movie prediction
jokes.punchline: Self-identification with 'swarthy' description
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor showing Liz's acceptance of unflattering but accurate description

jokes.text: My name is Joyce. I'm 63 years young. My favorite move is the Dougie.
jokes.setup: Older dance troupe member introducing herself
jokes.punchline: Elderly woman claims modern hip-hop dance as specialty
jokes.explanation: Decent age-inappropriate dance reference. Straightforward generational humor but well-executed.

jokes.text: I'm Liz. I'm 39, for the third time, and my favorite move is sunset arms.
jokes.setup: Liz introducing herself to dance troupe
jokes.punchline: Admits to lying about age and has made-up dance move
jokes.explanation: Good Liz character moment - reveals vanity and awkwardness. 'Sunset arms' sounds appropriately made-up.
